subject: GRB 230419A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely SHORT GRB

At 13:37:43 UT on 19 Apr 2023, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 230419A (trigger 703604268.398824 / 230419568).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 322.4, Dec = -19.1 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 21h 29m, -19d 06'), with a statistical uncertainty of 26.0 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 132.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2023/bn230419568/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn230419568.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2023/bn230419568/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn230419568.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2023/bn230419568/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn230419568.gif
